---
title: "RSEOps In Action"
subtitle: "Building Measurable, Reproducible, and Scalable Research Infrastructure"
author: "Tinashe M. Tapera"
date: "2026-07-08"
format:
  revealjs:
    theme: dracula
    slide-number: true
    incremental: false
    chalkboard: false
    code-fold: false
    transition: fade
    footer: "RSEOps In Action"
    reference-location: document
    title-slide-attributes: 
      # data-background-image: "PHOTO-2026-05-13-13-58-08.jpg"
      data-background-opacity: 30%
    embed-resources: true
execute:
  echo: false
  warning: false
  message: false
---

## Agenda

<!-- review the agenda for the day -->

## Why Should I Care?
<!-- 
[ 5 mins ]
Some motivating context for why we should care about RSEOps:
- What it means to "do science" from the perspective of a support team
  - It can look different from the perspective of a PI or Student
  - How do we align the goals?
- How having no process or infrastructure can hurt
  - You want to move as fast as possible with as little friction as possible
  - we can predict where you will have trouble, and so we impose structure and friction at the beginning, to help you avoid friction later
  - At best, later friction is a waste of time; at worst, it's a paper retraction or a lost career opportunity
 -->

## What is RSEOps?
<!-- 
[ 10 mins ]
- broadly, operationalize RSEOps and what we want to achieve
- Give examples of how NSAPH has already implemented some Ops principles
- Outline that there is no one-size-fits-all, there is no currently infallible authority (eg in medicine/pharma everybody knows how to report studies)
- Much of this we have to build ourselves, which is where the data team comes in
 -->

## Example 1: Stagecoach
<!-- 
[ 10 mins ]
- https://goldenplanetaryhealthlab.github.io/stagecoach/
 -->

## Example 2: Data Promotion
<!-- 
[ 10 mins ]
- play doh vs lego
- golden lab: gold mine
 -->

## Conclusion

<!-- 
[5 mins]
- this is how we operationalize RSEOps
- here is how you can help 
-->
